trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Isn't it gorgeous? It's my most efficient corpse eliminator! One head bites while the other chews." — Glogus, Golgari leechbreeder. [Scryfall] Glogus is named only here in MKM; the leechbreeder title signals a specialist role within the Golgari guild's corpse-processing hierarchy — consistent with Golgari lore where guild members specialize in various aspects of the death-and-renewal cycle.

Slimy Dualleech is typed Creature — Leech (no 'Golgari' subtype, but black-green color combination places it firmly in Golgari space). The Golgari Swarm's role in Ravnica's undercity — consuming the dead and processing waste — makes a two-headed leech functioning as a 'corpse eliminator' a naturalistic guild tool, not a horror aberration. [Scryfall]

The ability targets a creature you control with power 2 or less, granting +1/+0 and deathtouch until end of turn at the start of each combat. This synergizes with deathtouch-enabler strategies and small-creature aggro in black-green shells. EDHREC lists it in approximately 106 Commander decks as of 2025, with noted synergies in small-creature deathtouch packages. [EDHREC: slimy-dualleech]

The dual-headed leech design (two circular toothed mouths on segmented bodies) is thematically paired with a two-body, two-color card that buffs another creature — the creature's dual heads reflected in the card's dual-role function (own body + support effect). Art by Igor Krstic. [Scryfall]
